content.title: FollowMyHealth patient portal registration
content.text: FollowMyHealth gives 24/7 access to your medical records.

content.title: Accepting portal access
content.text: Completing this form allows direct access to your health history and a clinical summary of physician office visits.

content.title: Registration invitation
content.text: You will receive an email invitation to register for FollowMyHealth within the next 2 business days. If the online service is not activated within 90 days, the invitation will expire.

fields.label: I authorize creation or update of my patient portal account.

content.title: Original paper form
content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.

name: Patient Portal Access - Ages 12-17
description: Portal request for adolescent patients with privacy-aware consent.

content.title: Young adult full proxy access
content.text: This request gives another authorized person access to the patient medical record through FollowMyHealth.

content.title: Authorization to release protected health information
items: Proxy access lets another authorized person access medical information through FollowMyHealth.
Proxy access for an adult patient is valid until revoked by the patient.
Proxy access for a child account is valid until the child turns 12. After age 12, proxy permission must be authorized by the patient.

Information released through portal access may include sensitive information, including sexually transmitted disease, HIV/AIDS, behavioral health, mental health, and alcohol or drug treatment information.
Authorization is voluntary and may be refused.
Authorization may be revoked in writing, but revocation does not apply to information already released.
Information disclosed through portal access may be re-disclosed and may no longer be protected by federal and state privacy rules.
The authorization must be complete, signed, and dated. Portal proxy activation must occur within 30 days from the authorization date.

fields.label: I understand adolescent portal access may be limited by privacy rules.

content.title: Original paper form
content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.

name: Patient Portal Access - Under 11
description: Portal account request for children under 11.

content.title: FollowMyHealth proxy access
content.text: This request gives another authorized person access to the patient medical record through FollowMyHealth.

content.title: Authorization to release protected health information
items: Proxy access lets another authorized person access medical information through FollowMyHealth.
Proxy access for an adult patient is valid until revoked by the patient.
Proxy access for a child account is valid until the child turns 12. After age 12, proxy permission must be authorized by the patient.

Information released through portal access may include sensitive information, including sexually transmitted disease, HIV/AIDS, behavioral health, mental health, and alcohol or drug treatment information.
Authorization is voluntary and may be refused.
Authorization may be revoked in writing, but revocation does not apply to information already released.
Information disclosed through portal access may be re-disclosed and may no longer be protected by federal and state privacy rules.
The authorization must be complete, signed, and dated. Portal proxy activation must occur within 30 days from the authorization date.

fields.label: I request portal access and confirm I am authorized to act for this patient.

content.title: Original paper form
content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.

name: Release of Information
description: Authorization to release or request medical information.

content.title: Instructions
content.text: Please complete all sections legibly. Incomplete forms may result in delay or denial of this request.

content.title: Types of records
items: All health records, not including billing
Lab reports
Billing records
Office notes
Immunization record
Mental health records
Other records specified by the patient

content.title: Reason for request
items: Personal use
Disability
Insurance
Legal
Transfer care
Continuing care

content.title: Return completed forms to PYAM
items: Mail: Pediatric and Young Adult Medicine, 1804 7th Street W., Ste 200, Saint Paul, MN 55116
Email: medicalrecords@pyam.com
Drop off: Any PYAM location

content.title: Processing note
content.text: Records will be mailed to the person or organization identified in the recipient section. Please allow up to 2 weeks for processing.

content.title: Authorization acknowledgement
items: I may revoke this authorization at any time by notifying the identified facility in writing.
Once protected health information is released, it may no longer be protected and may potentially be re-disclosed.
There may be a fee for release of information, and I may be responsible for that fee.
Treatment will not be denied if I do not sign this form.
This authorization expires one year from the signature date unless otherwise specified.
If signed by someone other than the patient, legal documentation showing guardianship or authorization must be on file or presented with the form.

content.title: Signature requirement
content.text: Electronic typed signatures cannot be accepted on the source ROI form. Confirm clinic policy before accepting an electronic signature for this digital workflow.

content.title: Original paper form
content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.

name: Well Child Visit
description: General well visit intake that can be copied for age-specific forms.

content.title: Before you begin
content.text: Please complete this intake as accurately as possible. Required fields are marked with an asterisk. The clinic may review your answers with you during the visit.

fields.label: Recent ER, urgent care, hospital, or specialist visits

content.title: Visit preparation
content.text: Use this section to tell the clinic what has changed since the last visit and what concerns should be addressed today.

content.title: Age-specific guidance pending
content.text: The paper packet may include age-specific guidance, screening prompts, or educational content. This digital template is ready to hold that content once it is OCRed or transcribed.

content.title: Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale instructions
content.text: Answer each question based on how you have felt and conducted yourself over the past 6 months. Choose the response that best describes your experience.

content.title: Response options
items: Never
Rarely
Sometimes
Often
Very often

content.title: Clinical review
content.text: Please discuss the completed checklist with your healthcare professional during the appointment.

fields.label: Trouble wrapping up final details of a project
Difficulty getting things in order for a task
Avoiding or delaying tasks that require a lot of thought
Feeling overly active or driven by a motor

content.title: Additional ASRS questions
content.text: These questions complete the ASRS symptom checklist for clinician review.

fields.label: How often do you make careless mistakes when you have to work on a boring or difficult project?
How often do you have difficulty keeping your attention when you are doing boring or repetitive work?
How often do you have difficulty concentrating on what people say to you, even when they are speaking to you directly?
How often do you misplace or have difficulty finding things at home or at work?
How often are you distracted by activity or noise around you?
How often do you leave your seat in meetings or other situations in which you are expected to remain seated?
How often do you feel restless or fidgety?
How often do you have difficulty unwinding and relaxing when you have time to yourself?
How often do you find yourself talking too much when you are in social situations?
How often do you finish the sentences of people you are talking to before they can finish them themselves?
How often do you have difficulty waiting your turn in situations when turn taking is required?
How often do you interrupt others when they are busy?

content.title: Original paper form
content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.

name: Initial Anxiety Screener - Ages 6-12
description: Initial anxiety intake for children ages 6 through 12.

content.title: Screen for Child Anxiety Related Disorders (SCARED)
content.text: This packet includes a child version and a parent version. Each statement should be answered based on the last 3 months.

content.title: Response options
items: 0 - Not true or hardly ever true
1 - Somewhat true or sometimes true
2 - Very true or often true

content.title: For children ages 8 to 11
content.text: The source form recommends that the clinician explain all questions or have the child answer while sitting with an adult in case the child has questions.

fields.label: How long has this been happening?
Any safety concerns or self-harm statements?

content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.

content.title: About this test
content.text: The Asthma Control Test provides a numerical score to help assess asthma control and should be discussed with the doctor.

fields.label: Asthma kept you from getting as much done at work, school, or home
How often have you had shortness of breath?
Symptoms woke you up at night or early morning
How would you rate your asthma control?

content.title: Scoring note
content.text: If the total score is 19 or less, asthma may not be under control. Be sure to talk with the doctor about the results.

content.title: Answer scale
content.text: Each of the 5 scored questions is answered from least controlled to most controlled. Scores are intended to be totaled for clinical review.

fields.label: Emergency department visits for asthma in past 12 months
Oral steroid courses for asthma in past 12 months

content.title: Do not add to total score
content.text: Emergency department visits and hospitalizations are discussed with the doctor but are not added to the ACT total score.

content.title: Original paper form
content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.

name: Childhood Asthma Control Test - Ages 4-11
description: C-ACT screener for children ages 4 through 11.

content.title: How to take the Childhood Asthma Control Test
items: Step 1: Let the child respond to questions 1 through 4. You may help with reading or understanding, but let the child select the response.
Complete questions 5 through 7 on your own without letting the child response influence your answers.
There are no right or wrong answers.
Step 2: Record each answer score.
Step 3: Add the score boxes for the total.
Step 4: Take the test to the doctor to discuss the total score.

fields.label: How much of a problem is asthma when you run, exercise, or play sports?
Do you cough because of your asthma?
Do you wake up during the night because of your asthma?
Daytime asthma symptoms in the past 4 weeks
Woke up at night because of asthma in the past 4 weeks

content.title: Scoring note
content.text: If the child score is 19 or less, it may be a sign that asthma is not controlled as well as it could be. Bring the test to the doctor to discuss the results.

content.title: Original paper form
content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.

name: Acute Concussion Evaluation
description: Concussion injury characteristics and symptom checklist.

content.title: Clinician form context
content.text: The Acute Concussion Evaluation is intended for clinician office use and documents injury characteristics, reporter, mechanism, amnesia, loss of consciousness, early signs, and seizure details.

content.title: Symptom checklist instruction
content.text: Since the injury, indicate whether each symptom is present more than usual today or in the past day. The source form groups symptoms as physical, cognitive, emotional, and sleep symptoms.

content.title: Follow-up planning content from source form
items: Assess risk factors for prolonged recovery.
Record concussion history and headache history.
Consider relevant developmental, psychiatric, learning, sleep, and family history factors.
Document diagnosis, recommendations, and follow-up plan.

content.title: Original paper form
content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.

content.title: Consent for wart treatment
content.text: The patient has been diagnosed with a wart or molluscum contagiosum. Please review the treatment acknowledgements before signing.

fields.label: I understand possible risks, discomfort, blistering, pigment change, scarring, recurrence, or need for repeat treatment.

content.title: Treatment acknowledgements
items: There is no single treatment that can guarantee successful treatment of warts.
Treatment may require one or more methods or combinations of several methods.
Multiple treatments and multiple office visits may be required.
The treated area may develop new lesions, recurrences of previously treated lesions, or scarring.
Signing represents willingness to proceed with the procedure while understanding these statements.

content.title: Cost and insurance acknowledgement
items: In-office treatment is a surgical procedure and is billed accordingly.
Costs not covered by insurance are the responsibility of the guarantor.
An office visit is charged only if the provider is also evaluating or managing care not related to wart treatment.
Questions about cost may be discussed with the business office at 952-512-5625.
Co-payments, unmet deductibles, non-coverage, or other insurance balances are the responsibility of the guarantor.

content.title: Original paper form
content.text: For reference, you may open the original paper packet after completing the digital form.
